title: narcissus pseudonarcissus subsp. macrolobus
intro: Narcissus pseudonarcissus subsp. macrolobus, commonly known as the wild daffodil or large-flowered narcissus, is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the Amaryllidaceae family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Narcissus pseudonarcissus subsp. macrolobus include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and boost the immune system. The plant can be prepared into various herbal preparations, such as tinctures, infusions, and salves, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems.
